subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Know who tribes and nomads were
Understand why people started farming and living in villages
Learn about daily life, food, and customs in early communities
See how people traded and helped each other

lesson_plan.title: Who Were Tribes and Nomads?
student_activities: Look at pictures of tribes and nomads
Name some tribes or nomads in India
Share one interesting fact about them

lesson_plan.title: Food and Tools
topics_covered: How tribes hunted, gathered, and reared animals
teaching_strategy: Show pictures or simple replicas of tools
Ask students to guess how tools were used

lesson_plan.title: Settled Life
topics_covered: Villages instead of moving all the time
teaching_strategy: Storytelling and simple timeline on board
Show images of villages and farms
student_activities: List some early crops and animals

lesson_plan.title: Daily Life and Culture
teaching_strategy: Ask simple questions about daily life
student_activities: Draw or describe a festival or custom
Share a story about community life

lesson_plan.title: Trade and Helping Each Other
student_activities: Simulate a small trade between groups

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Fun Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of tribes, nomads, and villages
Relate past life to students’ own life
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and multiple choice
student_activities: Draw or share one thing they learned

teaching_aids: Pictures of tribes, nomads, and villages
Simple props or replicas of tools
Charts and maps for visual learning
teacher_tip: Keep the lesson visual and interactive. Use stories, drawings, and pictures. Encourage students to talk about what they see and imagine.
